Net '/6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1' is labelled with multiple names: /6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1, /b45054da-bad8-42d4-bf23-c90e1679d711/AIN1. KiCad treats this as one electrical net, but a future refactor that renames one label without the other silently decouples the two halves.
findings.recommendation: Pick the canonical name and remove the other labels, OR document the alias intentionally (e.g. an expressly named test point). If this is a cross-sheet connection via both global and hierarchical labels, prefer one or the other style consistently.

report_context.impact: Maintainability — silent alias across future edits.

findings.description: IC-based ESD protection device detected

findings.summary: IC power pin U3.14 (VINT) has no DC path to a power rail (net '__unnamed_19')
findings.description: Pin U3.14 (VINT) is type=power_in on net '__unnamed_19'. Graph walk (≤2 hops, rejecting capacitor edges, accepting resistors≤1Ω, inductors, ferrite beads, bridged solder jumpers) did not reach a named power rail. The pin is likely AC-coupled to ground only — the IC will not power up reliably.
findings.recommendation: Verify the schematic for this pin: the DC route must go through a conducting element (direct wire, 0Ω resistor, inductor, ferrite bead, or bridged solder jumper) — not a capacitor. If the intent was to tie VCC through an LC filter, add the missing inductor or 0Ω in series.

report_context.impact: IC supply floats DC; board will not run.

findings.summary: U1 pin INT (__unnamed_8) missing pull-up resistor
findings.description: Pin INT on U1 (MMC5983MA) is connected to net __unnamed_8 but has no pull-up resistor. This pin type typically requires an external pull-up to a power rail for correct operation.
findings.recommendation: Add a 4.7k-10k pull-up resistor from __unnamed_8 to the appropriate power rail.

fix_params.basis: Pin INT is open-drain/input type requiring pull-up

report_context.impact: Pin may float or bus may not function without pull-up

findings.description: low-pass RC filter
Net VBUS carries power_in pins but has no power_out pin, no PWR_FLAG, no regulator output mapping, and no bridged solder jumper path to a sourced net.
findings.recommendation: Add a PWR_FLAG to declare the rail as externally powered (e.g. from a connector) or trace the rail back to a regulator output. If the source lives on another sheet, promote the net name to a global label.

report_context.impact: Rail has no source visible to the analyser; likely a wiring gap or missing PWR_FLAG.

findings.summary: Sourcing nearly complete: 50/59 unique parts have MPNs (85%). Fill in remaining lines.
findings.description: Sourcing audit: 50 of 59 unique BOM lines (grouped by value + footprint) carry a manufacturer part number (84.7%). Pre-fab readiness requires 100% coverage; production assembly requires all parts sourceable via one of the supported distributors.
findings.recommendation: Populate the MPN field on the listed refs (use the digikey / mouser / lcsc / element14 skill to search by value/footprint when the MPN isn't already known).

report_context.impact: Pre-fab gate: board cannot be ordered until MPN coverage reaches 100%.

findings.summary: Net /6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1: 6.0V / 3.3V domain crossing without level shifter
findings.description: Net /6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1 connects ICs in 6.0V and 3.3V domains. The 6.0V output may exceed the 3.3V input absolute maximum rating of 3.6V, risking damage.
findings.recommendation: Add a level shifter between the 6.0V and 3.3V domains on net /6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1, or verify that the connected ICs have tolerant inputs.

fix_params.basis: Net crosses 6.0V to 3.3V boundary

report_context.impact: Risk of damage or unreliable logic levels

top_issues.summary: IC power pin U3.14 (VINT) has no DC path to a power rail (net '__unnamed_19')
top_issues.description: Pin U3.14 (VINT) is type=power_in on net '__unnamed_19'. Graph walk (≤2 hops, rejecting capacitor edges, accepting resistors≤1Ω, inductors, ferrite beads, bridged solder jumpers) did not reach a named power rail. The pin is likely AC-coupled to ground only — the IC will not power up reliably.
top_issues.recommendation: Verify the schematic for this pin: the DC route must go through a conducting element (direct wire, 0Ω resistor, inductor, ferrite bead, or bridged solder jumper) — not a capacitor. If the intent was to tie VCC through an LC filter, add the missing inductor or 0Ω in series.

report_context.impact: IC supply floats DC; board will not run.

top_issues.summary: Net /6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1: 6.0V / 3.3V domain crossing without level shifter
top_issues.description: Net /6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1 connects ICs in 6.0V and 3.3V domains. The 6.0V output may exceed the 3.3V input absolute maximum rating of 3.6V, risking damage.
top_issues.recommendation: Add a level shifter between the 6.0V and 3.3V domains on net /6fd78103-703b-49c2-b552-015594035fd1/AIN1, or verify that the connected ICs have tolerant inputs.

fix_params.basis: Net crosses 6.0V to 3.3V boundary

report_context.impact: Risk of damage or unreliable logic levels

top_issues.summary: U1 pin INT (__unnamed_8) missing pull-up resistor
top_issues.description: Pin INT on U1 (MMC5983MA) is connected to net __unnamed_8 but has no pull-up resistor. This pin type typically requires an external pull-up to a power rail for correct operation.
top_issues.recommendation: Add a 4.7k-10k pull-up resistor from __unnamed_8 to the appropriate power rail.

fix_params.basis: Pin INT is open-drain/input type requiring pull-up

report_context.impact: Pin may float or bus may not function without pull-up

top_issues.summary: VBUS has no declared source
top_issues.recommendation: Add a PWR_FLAG to declare the rail as externally powered (e.g. from a connector) or trace the rail back to a regulator output. If the source lives on another sheet, promote the net name to a global label.

manager.one_liner: 6 errors, 11 warnings across 3 categories. Schematic stage has blockers.
